defect: Price-time priority (I4/D3) violated: a later-arriving order at the same price level jumps ahead of an earlier resting order. This is the static analogue of the C2 concurrency race (SPIKE-FINDINGS trade-index-0: takerId=7 jumped ahead of takerId=3).
how_the_gate_rejects: The differential gate replays `orders` through the correct matcher and diffs the real output against this fixture's `corrupted_trades`. The corrupted sequence reorders the first two fills so a same-price later order matches first; the diff reports the first divergence index and REJECTS. If a gate accepts this fixture it is only checking per-trade validity (each corrupted trade is individually legal) and is a false-green.
why_per_trade_checks_miss_it: Both corrupted trades satisfy I1 (price 99 = resting price), I2 (buyQty==sellQty), I3 (net-zero), C1 (no over-fill). Only whole-sequence ordering (taker 2 arrived before taker 3, FIFO at same price) reveals the defect.
